key_insight: The paper reframes LLM cognition as a constitutive system where “structural tension” and topology-preserving plasticity drive native self-organization beyond prompt-level simulation.
engineering_relevance: It suggests a governance-first architecture for safe adaptive AI, where inference-time reconfiguration can improve internal consistency without changing pretrained weights.
method: Define an endogenous loss called Structural Tension from conflicts between new inputs and existing manifold topology.
Add an Offline Recurrent Loop to let the model self-process and stabilize conflicts without external input.
Enable Inference-time Plasticity with reversible, auditable topology changes under continuity constraints.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports a theoretical framework with operational definitions, minimal reconfiguration operators, falsification criteria, and a worked example, but no empirical dataset or benchmark results.
watch_item: The approach depends on strong governance invariants and may be difficult to validate or implement robustly without clear empirical tests of reversibility and safety.
paper_title: From Application-Layer Simulation to Native Meta-Architecture: Structural Tension as an Endogenous Driver for Heterogeneous AI Evolution
